claims.claim: We present a reproducible C microbenchmark evaluating how memory topology affects linked-cycle traversal throughput on an NVIDIA GB10 system operating under a single-node UMA memory model.

evidence_refs.quote: # Memory Topology Arena: Contiguous Index vs. Scattered Pointer Traversal on GB10-Class UMA Hardware

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: Three topologies are compared: (1) a contiguous arena with sequential index traversal, (2) a contiguous arena with random-cycle index traversal, and (3) a scattered `malloc`-per-node pointer graph with the same random-cycle structure.

evidence_refs.quote: # Memory Topology Arena: Contiguous Index vs. Scattered Pointer Traversal on GB10-Class UMA Hardware

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: At 10 million nodes, sequential arena traversal achieves 1.75 ns/step (570 Msteps/s), while random-cycle arena traversal degrades to 107 ns/step (9.35 Msteps/s)—a 61× slowdown.

evidence_refs.quote: # Memory Topology Arena: Contiguous Index vs. Scattered Pointer Traversal on GB10-Class UMA Hardware

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: The malloc pointer topology adds a further 1.12× slowdown at this scale and consumes 3.64× the resident memory.

evidence_refs.quote: # Memory Topology Arena: Contiguous Index vs. Scattered Pointer Traversal on GB10-Class UMA Hardware

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: At intermediate scales (1M nodes), however, the malloc penalty is 4.87×, indicating that allocator overhead and TLB pressure dominate before the working set fully exceeds cache.

claims.claim: These results confirm that memory locality dominates traversal throughput on this UMA host, and that contiguous arena indexing provides meaningful memory savings and throughput gains for random-cycle traversals at small-to-medium scales, but cannot compensate for the loss of algorithmic locality at very large working sets.

evidence_refs.quote: # Memory Topology Arena: Contiguous Index vs. Scattered Pointer Traversal on GB10-Class UMA Hardware

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: Prefetchers, cache hierarchies, and TLBs reward contiguous and predictable access, while pointer-chasing through scattered allocations penalizes throughput.

claims.claim: This study asks: can a small local arena benchmark produce actionable evidence about memory topology effects on GB10-class UMA hardware, specifically whether contiguous arena/index topologies outperform scattered pointer topologies under equivalent linked-cycle traversal?

claims.claim: We implement a minimal C11 microbenchmark comparing three traversal topologies over the same logical cycle structure, measure wall-clock throughput and resident memory across four problem sizes, and supplement with ARM PMU hardware counter data.
